meaning: <日>合气道(日本的一种徒手自卫术,利用对方的力气取胜)
examples: He concluded with an alternative strategy based on an Aikido phrase.
While she trained, her husband did Aikido and their daughters entertained themselves in the gym's nursery.
The problem with both of these approaches is that Aikido for O-Sensei was a process which continued right until he died.
In the case of aikido, we co-opt the attack to keep everyone as safe as possible.
Aikido-type blending with the energy coming towards one, whether a physical or emotional attack, is the opposite of surrender.
